items.title: Muse Soar to No. 1 on U.K.’s Albums Chart with ‘The Wow! Signal’
items.summary: It's the Devon-formed group's eighth U.K. chart-topper.

items.title: Sam Fender & Olivia Dean Close In on All-Time Records as ‘Rein Me In’ Earns 15th Week as U.K. No. 1
items.summary: The song is now tied for the longest No. 1 by a British act in U.K. Chart history.

items.title: The 5 Best Songs Of The Week
items.summary: Every week the Stereogum staff chooses the five best new songs of the week. The eligibility period begins and ends Thursdays right before midnight. You can hear this week’s picks below and on Stereogum’s Favorite New Music Spotify playlist, which is updated weekly.

items.title: Bob Vylan Sue BBC For Defamation
items.summary: Bob Vylan, the London punk-rap duo whose members go by Bobby Vylan and Bobbie Vylan, were at the center of controversy last summer after leading a "Death to the IDF!" chant at the UK's premier music festival, Glastonbury, in protest of Israel's genocidal war crimes in Palestine. At the time, the BBC, which livestreamed the festival, described the chant as "deeply offensive" and refused to make Bob Vylan's set viewable on demand.

items.title: Madonna’s Confessions II Is Here
items.summary: Conventional wisdom suggests the last great Madonna album was 2005's Confessions On A Dance Floor, a disco-opera of sorts created with producer Stuart Price to function like a full DJ set.

items.title: Saint Vitus Bar reopening in new Bushwick location

items.title: Our 50 Favorite Albums of 2026 So Far
items.summary: From Bill Callahan to Boards of Canada to Sluice to Soft Curse to Wendy Eisenberg to Olivia Rodrigo, here are our favorite albums of 2026 so far.

items.title: Win a Bon Scott 80th Birthday Prize Pack
YOSHIKI Begins His Boldest Chapter at Walt Disney Concert Hall
items.summary: If you had walked onstage at an X Japan concert at the height of the band’s chaotic 1980s/90s rise and told the sea of devoted fans that their ferocious, skin-bashing drummer YOSHIKI would one day headline Walt Disney Concert Hall, you would have been laughed out of the venue. But destiny has a funny way […]

items.title: The Music Business Has a New Music Problem
items.summary: July 1st marks halftime of 2026. What a disruptive year it’s been. I touched on it in Q1’s recap.  But in Q2, the era of great transformation is only accelerating, and success increasingly comes from a blatant disregard for the old way of doing things. There is more noise than ever before. More content, more […]

items.title: 5 Albums I Can’t Live Without: Jaret Reddick of Bowling for Soup
items.summary: Name  Jaret Reddick Best known for  Pop-punk Icon / Phineas and Ferb guy. Current city  Prosper, Texas. Really want to be in  I just wish I had a lake by my current location!  Excited about  Touring all the rest of 2026 and new music in 2027!!

items.title: The Guest List tackle modern masculinity on sweeping single ‘If Ever Your Devil Is Kind’
items.summary: "This song is about feeling unfulfilled in spite of having achieved everything that you thought would make you happy" The post The Guest List tackle modern masculinity on sweeping single ‘If Ever Your Devil Is Kind’ appeared first on NME.
